schema:
Table customers:
- id (INT, PK)
- name (TEXT)
- city (TEXT)

Table orders:
- id (INT, PK)
- customer_id (INT, FK)
- order_date (DATE)
- total (DECIMAL)
question: Show the names of customers from 'New York' who placed orders totaling more than $500.
sql: SELECT DISTINCT c.name FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id WHERE c.city = 'New York' AND o.total > 500;

schema:

- name (TEXT)
- salary (DECIMAL)
- dept_id (INT, FK)

Table departments:
- id (INT, PK)
- dept_name (TEXT)
question: What is the average salary for each department?
sql: SELECT d.dept_name, AVG(e.salary) FROM departments d JOIN employees e ON d.id = e.dept_id GROUP BY d.dept_name;

schema:

- category_id (INT, FK)

Table categories:
- id (INT, PK)
- category_name (TEXT)

Table stock:
- product_id (INT, FK)
- quantity (INT)
question: List all product names and their categories for items that are out of stock (quantity is 0).
sql: SELECT p.name, c.category_name FROM products p JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.id JOIN stock s ON p.id = s.product_id WHERE s.quantity = 0;

schema:
Table authors:

- name (TEXT)

Table books:
- id (INT, PK)
- author_id (INT, FK)
- title (TEXT)
- price (DECIMAL)
question: Which author has the highest total book sales value?
sql: SELECT a.name FROM authors a JOIN books b ON a.id = b.author_id GROUP BY a.id, a.name ORDER BY SUM(b.price) DESC LIMIT 1;

question: Find the names of students enrolled in 'Quantum Physics' with a grade above 85.
sql: SELECT s.name FROM students s JOIN enrollments e ON s.id = e.student_id JOIN courses c ON e.course_id = c.id WHERE c.title = 'Quantum Physics' AND e.grade > 85;

question: List teams where the total points scored by all players exceeds 1000.
sql: SELECT t.team_name FROM teams t JOIN players p ON t.id = p.team_id GROUP BY t.id, t.team_name HAVING SUM(p.points_scored) > 1000;

schema:

- id (INT, PK)
- name (TEXT)

Table recalls:
- product_id (INT, FK)
- recall_date (DATE)
question: How many 'Toaster' products were recalled in 2022?
sql: SELECT COUNT(r.id) FROM recalls r JOIN products p ON r.product_id = p.id WHERE p.name ILIKE '%Toaster%' AND r.recall_date BETWEEN '2022-01-01' AND '2022-12-31';

schema:
Table users:
- id (INT, PK)
- name (TEXT)

Table subscriptions:
- user_id (INT, FK)
- auto_renew (BOOLEAN)
question: List names of users who have disabled auto-renew on their subscriptions.
sql: SELECT u.name FROM users u JOIN subscriptions s ON u.id = s.user_id WHERE s.auto_renew = FALSE;

schema:
Table clinics:
- id (INT, PK)
- name (TEXT)

Table waiting_times:
- clinic_id (INT, FK)
- mins (INT)
question: Find the clinic with the lowest average waiting time.
sql: SELECT c.name FROM clinics c JOIN waiting_times wt ON c.id = wt.clinic_id GROUP BY c.id, c.name ORDER BY AVG(wt.mins) ASC LIMIT 1;

schema:
Table authors:
- id (INT, PK)
- name (TEXT)

Table royalties:
- author_id (INT, FK)
- amount (DECIMAL)
question: What is the total royalties earned by author 'J.K. Rowling'?
sql: SELECT SUM(r.amount) FROM royalties r JOIN authors a ON r.author_id = a.id WHERE a.name = 'J.K. Rowling';

schema:
Table servers:
- id (INT, PK)
- data_center (TEXT)

Table disk_usage:
- server_id (INT, FK)
- used_pct (INT)
question: Show servers in the 'Dublin' data center that are using more than 90% of their disk space.
sql: SELECT s.id FROM servers s JOIN disk_usage du ON s.id = du.server_id WHERE s.data_center = 'Dublin' AND du.used_pct > 90;

schema:
Table courses:
- id (INT, PK)
- title (TEXT)

Table student_grades:
- course_id (INT, FK)
- grade_letter (TEXT)
question: How many students received an 'A' in 'Macroeconomics'?
sql: SELECT COUNT(sg.id) FROM student_grades sg JOIN courses c ON sg.course_id = c.id WHERE c.title = 'Macroeconomics' AND sg.grade_letter = 'A';

schema:
Table teams:
- id (INT, PK)
- mascot (TEXT)

Table fan_club:
- team_id (INT, FK)
- members_count (INT)
question: Which team mascot belongs to the team with the largest fan club?
sql: SELECT t.mascot FROM teams t JOIN fan_club fc ON t.id = fc.team_id ORDER BY fc.members_count DESC LIMIT 1;

schema:
Table departments:
- id (INT, PK)
- head_name (TEXT)

Table floor_plan:
- dept_id (INT, FK)
- floor_number (INT)
question: List department heads located on the '5th' floor.
sql: SELECT d.head_name FROM departments d JOIN floor_plan fp ON d.id = fp.dept_id WHERE fp.floor_number = 5;

schema:
Table employees:
- id (INT, PK)
- name (TEXT)
- dept_id (INT, FK)
- hire_date (DATE)

Table salaries:
- emp_id (INT, FK)
- amount (DECIMAL)
- effective_date (DATE)
question: Find the names of employees who received a salary higher than the average salary across the company in their most recent payment.
sql: SELECT e.name FROM employees e JOIN salaries s ON e.id = s.emp_id WHERE s.amount > (SELECT AVG(s2.amount) FROM salaries s2) AND s.effective_date = (SELECT MAX(s3.effective_date) FROM salaries s3 WHERE s3.emp_id = e.id);

schema:
Table library_books:
- book_id (INT, PK)
- title (TEXT)
- category (TEXT)

Table checkouts:
- checkout_id (INT, PK)
- book_id (INT, FK)
- member_id (INT)
- status (TEXT)
question: Which book categories have never had a book with a status of 'Lost'?
sql: SELECT DISTINCT lb.category FROM library_books lb WHERE lb.category NOT IN (SELECT lb2.category FROM library_books lb2 JOIN checkouts c ON lb2.book_id = c.book_id WHERE c.status = 'Lost');

schema:
Table products:
- id (INT, PK)
- name (TEXT)
- cost_price (DECIMAL)

Table sales_data:
- product_id (INT, FK)
- sale_price (DECIMAL)
- quantity (INT)
question: Calculate the total profit for each product, where profit is (sale_price - cost_price) * quantity.
sql: SELECT p.name, SUM((sd.sale_price - p.cost_price) * sd.quantity) AS total_profit FROM products p JOIN sales_data sd ON p.id = sd.product_id GROUP BY p.id, p.name;

schema:
Table hotel_rooms:
- room_id (INT, PK)
- room_type (TEXT)
- price (DECIMAL)

Table reservations:
- res_id (INT, PK)
- room_id (INT, FK)
- start_date (DATE)
- end_date (DATE)
question: Show the room IDs that were occupied for more than 20 days in total during the year 2023.
sql: SELECT r.room_id FROM reservations r WHERE r.start_date >= '2023-01-01' AND r.end_date <= '2023-12-31' GROUP BY r.room_id HAVING SUM(r.end_date - r.start_date) > 20;

schema:
Table artists:
- artist_id (INT, PK)
- name (TEXT)

Table song_plays:
- song_id (INT)
- artist_id (INT, FK)
- play_count (INT)
- region (TEXT)
question: List the artists whose songs have been played more than 1,000,000 times in 'Global' but less than 10,000 times in 'Local'.
sql: SELECT a.name FROM artists a JOIN song_plays sp1 ON a.artist_id = sp1.artist_id WHERE sp1.region = 'Global' GROUP BY a.artist_id, a.name HAVING SUM(sp1.play_count) > 1000000 INTERSECT SELECT a.name FROM artists a JOIN song_plays sp2 ON a.artist_id = sp2.artist_id WHERE sp2.region = 'Local' GROUP BY a.artist_id, a.name HAVING SUM(sp2.play_count) < 10000;

schema:
Table warehouses:
- w_id (INT, PK)
- region (TEXT)

Table inventory_logs:
- log_id (INT, PK)
- w_id (INT, FK)
- item_name (TEXT)
- stock_delta (INT)
question: Which warehouse regions had a net decrease in stock for 'Laptops' over the last 30 days?
sql: SELECT w.region FROM warehouses w JOIN inventory_logs il ON w.w_id = il.w_id WHERE il.item_name = 'Laptop' GROUP BY w.w_id, w.region HAVING SUM(il.stock_delta) < 0;

schema:
Table authors:
- id (INT, PK)
- name (TEXT)
- debut_year (INT)

Table publications:
- pub_id (INT, PK)
- author_id (INT, FK)
- format (TEXT)
question: Find authors who debuted before 2000 and have published at least one 'Digital' and one 'Print' format book.
sql: SELECT a.name FROM authors a JOIN publications p1 ON a.id = p1.author_id JOIN publications p2 ON a.id = p2.author_id WHERE a.debut_year < 2000 AND p1.format = 'Digital' AND p2.format = 'Print';

schema:
Table customers:
- c_id (INT, PK)
- name (TEXT)

Table support_cases:
- case_id (INT, PK)
- c_id (INT, FK)
- priority (TEXT)
- resolution_time_hrs (INT)
question: Identify customers who have an average resolution time for 'High' priority cases that is longer than 48 hours.
sql: SELECT c.name FROM customers c JOIN support_cases sc ON c.c_id = sc.c_id WHERE sc.priority = 'High' GROUP BY c.c_id, c.name HAVING AVG(sc.resolution_time_hrs) > 48;

schema:
Table courses:
- course_id (INT, PK)
- title (TEXT)

Table grades:
- student_id (INT)
- course_id (INT, FK)
- score (INT)
question: What is the highest score achieved in each course, and who (student_id) achieved it?
sql: SELECT g.course_id, c.title, g.student_id, g.score FROM grades g JOIN courses c ON g.course_id = c.course_id WHERE g.score = (SELECT MAX(g2.score) FROM grades g2 WHERE g2.course_id = g.course_id);

schema:
Table vehicles:
- id (INT, PK)
- owner_id (INT, FK)
- type (TEXT)

Table maintenance_jobs:
- job_id (INT, PK)
- vehicle_id (INT, FK)
- part_replaced (TEXT)
question: How many 'Truck' owners have never had a 'Transmission' replaced in their maintenance logs?
sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT v.owner_id) FROM vehicles v WHERE v.type = 'Truck' AND v.id NOT IN (SELECT mj.vehicle_id FROM maintenance_jobs mj WHERE mj.part_replaced = 'Transmission');

schema:
Table server_clusters:
- cluster_id (INT, PK)
- location (TEXT)

Table nodes:
- node_id (INT, PK)
- cluster_id (INT, FK)
- ram_utilization (DECIMAL)
question: Find the locations where all nodes in the cluster have a RAM utilization above 80%.
sql: SELECT sc.location FROM server_clusters sc WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM nodes n WHERE n.cluster_id = sc.cluster_id AND n.ram_utilization <= 80);

schema:
Table users:
- user_id (INT, PK)
- name (TEXT)

Table purchases:
- p_id (INT, PK)
- user_id (INT, FK)
- category (TEXT)
- price (DECIMAL)
question: Which users have spent more on 'Electronics' than they have on 'Groceries'?
sql: SELECT u.name FROM users u JOIN purchases p1 ON u.user_id = p1.user_id GROUP BY u.user_id, u.name HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN p1.category = 'Electronics' THEN p1.price ELSE 0 END) > SUM(CASE WHEN p1.category = 'Groceries' THEN p1.price ELSE 0 END);

schema:
Table property_agents:
- agent_id (INT, PK)
- name (TEXT)

Table listings:
- l_id (INT, PK)
- agent_id (INT, FK)
- neighborhood (TEXT)
- status (TEXT)
question: Show agents who have at least 5 'Sold' listings in 'Manhattan'.
sql: SELECT pa.name FROM property_agents pa JOIN listings l ON pa.agent_id = l.agent_id WHERE l.neighborhood = 'Manhattan' AND l.status = 'Sold' GROUP BY pa.agent_id, pa.name HAVING COUNT(l.l_id) >= 5;

schema:
Table companies:
- c_id (INT, PK)
- name (TEXT)

Table department_budgets:
- dept_name (TEXT)
- company_id (INT, FK)
- budget (DECIMAL)
question: What is the name of the company that has the largest single department budget?
sql: SELECT c.name FROM companies c JOIN department_budgets db ON c.c_id = db.company_id ORDER BY db.budget DESC LIMIT 1;

schema:
Table flight_routes:
- route_id (INT, PK)
- carrier (TEXT)

Table ticket_sales:
- sale_id (INT, PK)
- route_id (INT, FK)
- class (TEXT)
- revenue (DECIMAL)
question: Compare the total revenue of 'First' class vs 'Economy' class for carrier 'United'.
sql: SELECT ts.class, SUM(ts.revenue) FROM ticket_sales ts JOIN flight_routes fr ON ts.route_id = fr.route_id WHERE fr.carrier = 'United' GROUP BY ts.class;

schema:
Table projects:
- id (INT, PK)
- name (TEXT)

Table hours_logged:
- project_id (INT, FK)
- user_id (INT)
- hours (DECIMAL)
- work_date (DATE)
question: Find the total hours logged on project 'X' by each user during February 2024.
sql: SELECT hl.user_id, SUM(hl.hours) FROM hours_logged hl JOIN projects p ON hl.project_id = p.id WHERE p.name = 'X' AND hl.work_date BETWEEN '2024-02-01' AND '2024-02-29' GROUP BY hl.user_id;

schema:
Table products:
- sku (TEXT, PK)
- category (TEXT)

Table store_inventory:
- store_id (INT)
- sku (TEXT, FK)
- quantity (INT)
question: List categories where every item in that category has a total quantity across all stores greater than 100.
sql: SELECT p.category FROM products p JOIN store_inventory si ON p.sku = si.sku GROUP BY p.category HAVING MIN(si.quantity) > 100;

schema:
Table doctors:
- d_id (INT, PK)
- name (TEXT)

Table surgery_schedule:
- surgery_id (INT, PK)
- doctor_id (INT, FK)
- duration_mins (INT)
question: Which doctor has performed surgeries totaling more than 50 hours?
sql: SELECT d.name FROM doctors d JOIN surgery_schedule ss ON d.d_id = ss.doctor_id GROUP BY d.d_id, d.name HAVING SUM(ss.duration_mins) > 3000;

schema:
Table stream_users:
- id (INT, PK)
- name (TEXT)

Table content_history:
- user_id (INT, FK)
- content_type (TEXT)
- watch_time_mins (INT)
question: List users who spend at least 70% of their watch time on 'Documentary' content.
sql: SELECT su.name FROM stream_users su JOIN content_history ch ON su.id = ch.user_id GROUP BY su.id, su.name HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN ch.content_type = 'Documentary' THEN ch.watch_time_mins ELSE 0 END) >= 0.7 * SUM(ch.watch_time_mins);

schema:
Table cities:
- city_id (INT, PK)
- name (TEXT)

Table weather_stations:
- station_id (INT, PK)
- city_id (INT, FK)
- rainfall_mm (DECIMAL)
- date_recorded (DATE)
question: Find cities that experienced more than 500mm of total rainfall in the month of May 2023.
sql: SELECT c.name FROM cities c JOIN weather_stations ws ON c.city_id = ws.city_id WHERE ws.date_recorded BETWEEN '2023-05-01' AND '2023-05-31' GROUP BY c.city_id, c.name HAVING SUM(ws.rainfall_mm) > 500;

schema:
Table bank_clients:
- client_id (INT, PK)
- name (TEXT)

Table credit_cards:
- card_num (TEXT, PK)
- client_id (INT, FK)
- balance (DECIMAL)
- limit_amt (DECIMAL)
question: List names of clients whose total credit card balance across all cards exceeds 90% of their total credit limit.
sql: SELECT bc.name FROM bank_clients bc JOIN credit_cards cc ON bc.client_id = cc.client_id GROUP BY bc.client_id, bc.name HAVING SUM(cc.balance) > 0.9 * SUM(cc.limit_amt);

schema:
Table departments:
- dept_id (INT, PK)
- name (TEXT)

Table job_postings:
- job_id (INT, PK)
- dept_id (INT, FK)
- remote_eligible (BOOLEAN)
question: Count the number of remote-eligible jobs available in the 'Engineering' department.
sql: SELECT COUNT(jp.job_id) FROM job_postings jp JOIN departments d ON jp.dept_id = d.dept_id WHERE d.name = 'Engineering' AND jp.remote_eligible = TRUE;

schema:
Table sensors:
- s_id (INT, PK)
- location (TEXT)

Table alert_logs:
- alert_id (INT, PK)
- s_id (INT, FK)
- severity (INT)
question: Which sensor locations have generated more than 10 alerts with a severity of 5 or higher?
sql: SELECT s.location FROM sensors s JOIN alert_logs al ON s.s_id = al.s_id WHERE al.severity >= 5 GROUP BY s.s_id, s.location HAVING COUNT(al.alert_id) > 10;

schema:
Table restaurants:
- r_id (INT, PK)
- name (TEXT)

Table safety_ratings:
- r_id (INT, FK)
- score (INT)
- inspection_year (INT)
question: List restaurants that improved their safety score from 2022 to 2023.
sql: SELECT r.name FROM restaurants r JOIN safety_ratings sr1 ON r.r_id = sr1.r_id JOIN safety_ratings sr2 ON r.r_id = sr2.r_id WHERE sr1.inspection_year = 2022 AND sr2.inspection_year = 2023 AND sr2.score > sr1.score;

schema:
Table vehicle_models:
- m_id (INT, PK)
- manufacturer (TEXT)
- fuel_type (TEXT)

Table registrations:
- r_id (INT, PK)
- m_id (INT, FK)
- reg_year (INT)
question: How many 'Electric' vehicles were registered in 2023 by the manufacturer 'Tesla'?
sql: SELECT COUNT(r.r_id) FROM registrations r JOIN vehicle_models vm ON r.m_id = vm.m_id WHERE vm.fuel_type = 'Electric' AND vm.manufacturer = 'Tesla' AND r.reg_year = 2023;

schema:
Table teachers:
- t_id (INT, PK)
- name (TEXT)

Table student_feedback:
- feedback_id (INT, PK)
- teacher_id (INT, FK)
- rating (INT)
question: Find the teacher with the lowest average rating.
sql: SELECT t.name FROM teachers t JOIN student_feedback sf ON t.t_id = sf.teacher_id GROUP BY t.t_id, t.name ORDER BY AVG(sf.rating) ASC LIMIT 1;

schema:
Table events:
- e_id (INT, PK)
- name (TEXT)

Table ticket_orders:
- order_id (INT, PK)
- event_id (INT, FK)
- qty (INT)
- price_per_ticket (DECIMAL)
question: What is the total revenue for the event 'Glastonbury Festival'?
sql: SELECT SUM(to.qty * to.price_per_ticket) FROM ticket_orders to JOIN events e ON to.event_id = e.e_id WHERE e.name = 'Glastonbury Festival';

schema:
Table app_users:
- id (INT, PK)
- username (TEXT)

Table friends_list:
- user_id (INT, FK)
- friend_id (INT)
question: Which user has the most friends in the system?
sql: SELECT au.username FROM app_users au JOIN friends_list fl ON au.id = fl.user_id GROUP BY au.id, au.username ORDER BY COUNT(fl.friend_id) DESC LIMIT 1;

schema:
Table shipping_ports:
- p_id (INT, PK)
- city (TEXT)

Table container_logs:
- log_id (INT, PK)
- port_id (INT, FK)
- weight_tons (DECIMAL)
- operation (TEXT)
question: Calculate the net weight change (Inbound - Outbound) for the port in 'Singapore'.
sql: SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN cl.operation = 'Inbound' THEN cl.weight_tons ELSE -cl.weight_tons END) FROM container_logs cl JOIN shipping_ports sp ON cl.port_id = sp.p_id WHERE sp.city = 'Singapore';

schema:
Table stores:
- s_id (INT, PK)
- address (TEXT)

Table manager_history:
- store_id (INT, FK)
- manager_id (INT)
- tenure_days (INT)
question: Find the average tenure length of managers for store ID 101.
sql: SELECT AVG(mh.tenure_days) FROM manager_history mh WHERE mh.store_id = 101;

schema:
Table stock_items:
- id (INT, PK)
- name (TEXT)

Table price_history:
- item_id (INT, FK)
- price (DECIMAL)
- valid_from (DATE)
question: List items whose price has increased by more than 50% between their first recorded price and their most recent price.
sql: SELECT si.name FROM stock_items si JOIN price_history ph_first ON si.id = ph_first.item_id JOIN price_history ph_last ON si.id = ph_last.item_id WHERE ph_first.valid_from = (SELECT MIN(p1.valid_from) FROM price_history p1 WHERE p1.item_id = si.id) AND ph_last.valid_from = (SELECT MAX(p2.valid_from) FROM price_history p2 WHERE p2.item_id = si.id) AND ph_last.price > 1.5 * ph_first.price;

schema:
Table hospitals:
- h_id (INT, PK)
- name (TEXT)

Table departments:
- d_id (INT, PK)
- h_id (INT, FK)
- budget (DECIMAL)

Table staff:
- s_id (INT, PK)
- d_id (INT, FK)
- role (TEXT)
question: Count the total number of staff members in all hospitals that have a total department budget exceeding $10,000,000.
sql: SELECT COUNT(s.s_id) FROM staff s JOIN departments d ON s.d_id = d.d_id WHERE d.h_id IN (SELECT d2.h_id FROM departments d2 GROUP BY d2.h_id HAVING SUM(d2.budget) > 10000000);

schema:
Table countries:
- code (TEXT, PK)
- name (TEXT)

Table population_stats:
- country_code (TEXT, FK)
- year (INT)
- total_count (BIGINT)
question: Find the countries that experienced a population decline between 2010 and 2020.
sql: SELECT c.name FROM countries c JOIN population_stats p1 ON c.code = p1.country_code JOIN population_stats p2 ON c.code = p2.country_code WHERE p1.year = 2010 AND p2.year = 2020 AND p2.total_count < p1.total_count;

schema:
Table game_developers:
- dev_id (INT, PK)
- studio_name (TEXT)

Table game_titles:
- g_id (INT, PK)
- dev_id (INT, FK)
- platform (TEXT)
- score (INT)
question: Which studios have released games on 'Nintendo Switch' with an average score of 90 or higher?
sql: SELECT gd.studio_name FROM game_developers gd JOIN game_titles gt ON gd.dev_id = gt.dev_id WHERE gt.platform = 'Nintendo Switch' GROUP BY gd.dev_id, gd.studio_name HAVING AVG(gt.score) >= 90;

schema:
Table authors:
- a_id (INT, PK)
- name (TEXT)

Table books:
- b_id (INT, PK)
- author_id (INT, FK)
- page_count (INT)
question: What is the name of the author who has written the highest total number of pages across all books?
sql: SELECT a.name FROM authors a JOIN books b ON a.a_id = b.author_id GROUP BY a.a_id, a.name ORDER BY SUM(b.page_count) DESC LIMIT 1;

schema:
Table network_nodes:
- id (INT, PK)
- status (TEXT)

Table traffic_metrics:
- node_id (INT, FK)
- data_out_gb (DECIMAL)
- timestamp (TIMESTAMP)
question: Show 'Active' nodes that have transmitted more than 100GB of data in the last 24 hours.
sql: SELECT DISTINCT nn.id FROM network_nodes nn JOIN traffic_metrics tm ON nn.id = tm.node_id WHERE nn.status = 'Active' AND tm.timestamp >= NOW() - INTERVAL '1 day' GROUP BY nn.id HAVING SUM(tm.data_out_gb) > 100;

schema:
Table flight_pilots:
- pilot_id (INT, PK)
- name (TEXT)

Table certified_aircraft:
- pilot_id (INT, FK)
- model_name (TEXT)
question: How many pilots are certified to fly both 'Boeing 777' and 'Airbus A350'?
sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT ca.pilot_id FROM certified_aircraft ca WHERE ca.model_name = 'Boeing 777' INTERSECT SELECT ca2.pilot_id FROM certified_aircraft ca2 WHERE ca2.model_name = 'Airbus A350') AS certified_both;

schema:
Table publishers:
- id (INT, PK)
- name (TEXT)

Table magazine_issues:
- issue_id (INT, PK)
- publisher_id (INT, FK)
- circulation_count (INT)
question: Find the publisher with the highest total circulation across all issues.
sql: SELECT p.name FROM publishers p JOIN magazine_issues mi ON p.id = mi.publisher_id GROUP BY p.id, p.name ORDER BY SUM(mi.circulation_count) DESC LIMIT 1;

schema:
Table city_districts:
- district_id (INT, PK)
- name (TEXT)

Table crime_stats:
- district_id (INT, FK)
- year (INT)
- incident_count (INT)
question: Which district saw the greatest percentage reduction in crime between 2021 and 2022?
sql: SELECT cd.name FROM city_districts cd JOIN crime_stats cs1 ON cd.district_id = cs1.district_id JOIN crime_stats cs2 ON cd.district_id = cs2.district_id WHERE cs1.year = 2021 AND cs2.year = 2022 ORDER BY ((cs1.incident_count - cs2.incident_count) * 100.0 / cs1.incident_count) DESC LIMIT 1;

schema:
Table employees:
- id (INT, PK)
- name (TEXT)

Table project_leads:
- emp_id (INT, FK)
- project_count (INT)
question: Show the name of the employee who leads the highest number of projects.
sql: SELECT e.name FROM employees e JOIN project_leads pl ON e.id = pl.emp_id ORDER BY pl.project_count DESC LIMIT 1;

schema:
Table taxi_fleets:
- fleet_id (INT, PK)
- city (TEXT)

Table rides:
- ride_id (INT, PK)
- fleet_id (INT, FK)
- fare (DECIMAL)
question: What is the average fare for taxi rides in 'London'?
sql: SELECT AVG(r.fare) FROM rides r JOIN taxi_fleets tf ON r.fleet_id = tf.fleet_id WHERE tf.city = 'London';

schema:
Table software_apps:
- id (INT, PK)
- app_name (TEXT)

Table bug_reports:
- bug_id (INT, PK)
- app_id (INT, FK)
- severity (TEXT)
question: List application names that have zero 'Critical' bugs reported.
sql: SELECT sa.app_name FROM software_apps sa WHERE sa.id NOT IN (SELECT br.app_id FROM bug_reports br WHERE br.severity = 'Critical');

schema:
Table customer_orders:
- order_id (INT, PK)
- customer_id (INT)

Table shipping_status:
- order_id (INT, FK)
- status (TEXT)
- update_date (TIMESTAMP)
question: Find the order IDs that reached the 'Out for Delivery' status on the same day they were 'Shipped'.
sql: SELECT s1.order_id FROM shipping_status s1 JOIN shipping_status s2 ON s1.order_id = s2.order_id WHERE s1.status = 'Shipped' AND s2.status = 'Out for Delivery' AND s1.update_date::DATE = s2.update_date::DATE;

schema:
Table athletes:
- id (INT, PK)
- name (TEXT)
- country (TEXT)

Table medals:
- athlete_id (INT, FK)
- medal_type (TEXT)
- game_year (INT)
question: How many 'Gold' medals has the country 'Australia' won across all Olympic games in the database?
sql: SELECT COUNT(m.medal_type) FROM medals m JOIN athletes a ON m.athlete_id = a.id WHERE a.country = 'Australia' AND m.medal_type = 'Gold';

schema:
Table bank_accounts:
- acc_id (INT, PK)
- owner_id (INT)

Table balance_history:
- acc_id (INT, FK)
- balance (DECIMAL)
- record_date (DATE)
question: Show the owner ID and their final balance recorded on the most recent date available.
sql: SELECT ba.owner_id, bh.balance FROM bank_accounts ba JOIN balance_history bh ON ba.acc_id = bh.acc_id WHERE bh.record_date = (SELECT MAX(bh2.record_date) FROM balance_history bh2);

schema:
Table movies:
- m_id (INT, PK)
- title (TEXT)

Table streaming_stats:
- m_id (INT, FK)
- month (TEXT)
- hours_streamed (INT)
question: Which movie was streamed the most hours in 'January'?
sql: SELECT m.title FROM movies m JOIN streaming_stats ss ON m.m_id = ss.m_id WHERE ss.month = 'January' ORDER BY ss.hours_streamed DESC LIMIT 1;
